body.title: [triage] `UU_DB_PASSWORD` is stored in PLAINTEXT in ~/.unseen_university/uu_bash_profile.sh — move it to the vault device
body.intention: I intend that no live credential sits in plaintext in a file that every shell sources and every command echoes — because a secret that leaks into a transcript the moment anyone greps its file is not a secret, and the vault device already exists to be its home. [intention_date: 2026-07-13]
body.description:
**FOUND 2026-07-13** while resolving `INFERENCE_ENDPOINT` during the Hex diagnosis.

`~/.unseen_university/uu_bash_profile.sh` line 8 contains **`export UU_DB_PASSWORD=<plaintext>`**. The
file is sourced by the shell profile, so the password is in the environment of every process — and it
**leaked into this session's transcript** the moment I grepped the file for an unrelated variable.
That is the failure mode: it does not require an attacker, only a `grep`.

CLAUDE.md's hard rule is "no live keys or passwords in source" and the canonical credential home is the
**vault device** ([[reference_vault_is_the_credential_home]]). This is neither. (Note: the older memory
pointing at `igor.credentials.cfg` is stale — **that file does not exist**; `uu_bash_profile.sh` is
what actually sets the environment today. That memory should be corrected as part of this work.)

**I did not edit Akien's shell profile** — changing where a live credential lives is his call, not a
side effect of a debugging session.

**Scope boundary:** IN — move `UU_DB_PASSWORD` into the vault; have the DB URL composed at connect-time
from the vault ([[reference_compose_credentials_at_connect_time]]); leave the profile with no secret in
it. OUT — rotating the password (Akien's call); other credentials, unless the sweep finds them.

**Test plan:** grep the profile and the repo for the literal secret -> zero hits; a fresh shell with no
`UU_DB_PASSWORD` in the environment can still connect, because the URL is composed at connect-time.

**Completion criteria:**
  1. `grep -r <secret> ~/.unseen_university/ ~/dev/src/UnseenUniversity/` returns nothing.
  2. A shell with no `UU_DB_PASSWORD` exported still connects to the DB via the vault.
  3. The stale `igor.credentials.cfg` memory is corrected to name the real file.
